description: Plan 6 — Feedback, Resolution Memory + Account Pulse. Turn approved responses into reusable knowledge entries, surface accuracy and impact metrics in App Home, add /relay ask (ad-hoc retrieval) and /relay pulse (account health digest), and ship admin feedback export.

userStories.title: Add knowledge_entries migration and ORM model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the knowledge_entries table so resolution memory can be persisted and retrieved.
acceptanceCriteria: Alembic migration 0006_plan6_memory.py

knowledge_entries table: id (UUID PK), workspace_id (FK workspaces.id CASCADE), question_id (UUID nullable FK questions.id), title (TEXT), summary (TEXT), customer_question (TEXT), internal_answer (TEXT), source_bundle (JSONB), reuse_count (INT default 0), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ server_default now()); RLS ENABLE + FORCE
Index on knowledge_entries(workspace_id, created_at) for recency queries
Add indexes to impact_metrics(workspace_id, created_at) and feedback_signals(workspace_id, created_at, correction_action) if not present
ORM model KnowledgeEntry added to relay/db/models.py with workspace/question relationships and overlaps= silencing
alembic upgrade head runs cleanly on top of 0005
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: impact_metrics and feedback_signals tables were created in migration 0005. This migration adds knowledge_entries and the missing indexes. The knowledge_chunks table already has knowledge_entry_id UUID nullable column (added in 0004) — just add the FK constraint here.

userStories.title: Index approved responses as knowledge_entries
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every sent response to create a knowledge_entry so future drafts can cite prior answers.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/drafting/memory.py implements async index_approved_response(workspace_id, question_id, draft_id, session) -> KnowledgeEntry
Reads question (title_excerpt, channel_id), resolves account_id via MonitoredChannel, reads draft (customer_draft, internal_brief, evidence_bundle)
Creates KnowledgeEntry: customer_question=question.title_excerpt, internal_answer=draft.customer_draft, source_bundle=draft.evidence_bundle, title=account_name + ' — ' + question.title_excerpt[:80]
Generates one-sentence summary using claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (Haiku) with a simple 'Summarize in one sentence:' prompt
Embeds customer_question + '\n\n' + internal_answer via embed_chunks(), stores KnowledgeChunk with knowledge_entry_id set and source_document_id=None
Called from relay_send_draft handler after the response is posted (after ImpactMetric write)
Unit tests in tests/test_memory.py: verify KnowledgeEntry created, chunk embedded with correct knowledge_entry_id, summary is a non-empty string
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: embed_chunks() from relay.connectors.embeddings takes (workspace_id, chunks, connector_id, source_document_id, session). For knowledge entries: pass connector_id=None and source_document_id=None. Haiku model ID: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Keep the summary prompt minimal.

userStories.title: Retrieval cites knowledge_entry chunks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want retrieve() to surface prior approved answers as evidence with correct citation metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/connectors/retrieval.py citation output for chunks where knowledge_entry_id IS NOT NULL includes: provider='relay_memory', title from KnowledgeEntry.title, url=None
KnowledgeEntry.reuse_count incremented by 1 each time a chunk from that entry is included in a retrieval result
Unit test in tests/test_retrieval.py: create a KnowledgeChunk with knowledge_entry_id set, call retrieve(), verify citation has provider='relay_memory' and reuse_count incremented
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current retrieve() in retrieval.py returns RetrievedChunk with citation={}. To populate citation for knowledge_entry chunks, do a secondary lookup: after fetching chunks, load KnowledgeEntry rows for any chunk that has knowledge_entry_id set, then build citation from the KnowledgeEntry. Use a single SELECT IN query to avoid N+1.

userStories.title: /relay ask slash command — ad-hoc retrieval
userStories.description: As a CSM, I want to run /relay ask <text> to get immediate evidence retrieval without a full question workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/commands/ask.py implements handle_ask(ack, respond, command) Bolt handler
Parses query from command['text'] after stripping 'ask' prefix
If query empty: ephemeral 'Usage: /relay ask <your question>'
Resolves workspace_id from command['team_id']
Calls retrieve(workspace_id, query, session, top_k=5) inside a workspace-scoped session
Responds ephemeral with Block Kit sections: one per source (title, provider badge, URL link if available, excerpt truncated to 150 chars, freshness)
If zero results: 'No relevant sources found in connected knowledge base.'
relay/commands/help.py registers 'ask' subcommand
relay/slack/app.py (or relay/commands/__init__.py) imports the ask handler
Unit tests in tests/test_ask_command.py: empty query returns usage hint, with results returns formatted blocks, zero results returns no-sources message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The /relay command routes subcommands from command['text']. Currently handle_register is dispatched; add 'ask' routing. retrieve() is async — use asyncio.run() inside the Bolt sync handler OR ensure the Bolt app is fully async (it should be — use async def with await). Do NOT create Question/Alert/Draft rows.

userStories.title: App Home — impact metrics section
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see RELAY's 30-day SLA met rate, draft acceptance rate, and time-to-send in the App Home.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/slack/home.py gains _impact_blocks(impact_rows) pure helper

Shows rolling 30-day stats computed from impact_metrics: SLA met rate (%), draft accepted rate (%), median time_to_send_seconds formatted as human-readable (e.g. '4m 32s'), total questions handled (COUNT(*) of impact_metrics rows)
If len(impact_rows) == 0: single section 'No data yet — stats appear after your first sent response.'
build_home() accepts optional impact_rows: list[Any] = None parameter; appends _impact_blocks(impact_rows or []) after connector blocks
publish_app_home handler queries SELECT * FROM impact_metrics WHERE workspace_id=? AND created_at >= now()-30d LIMIT 500 before calling build_home
Unit test: build_home() with sample impact rows returns blocks containing SLA rate and draft accepted rate
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SLA met rate = sum(sla_met) / count(sla_met IS NOT NULL). Draft accepted rate = sum(draft_accepted) / count(*). Median: sort time_to_send_seconds, take the middle value. All pure Python from the list of rows — no aggregation SQL needed.

userStories.title: App Home — accuracy and feedback review section
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a 7-day accuracy summary in App Home showing false-positive rate and mark_not_question corrections.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/slack/home.py gains _accuracy_blocks(feedback_rows) pure helper
Shows rolling 7-day stats: correction count (correction_action='mark_not_question'), accuracy rate formula: (total_questions - corrections) / total_questions * 100%
Includes a 'Export feedback' button (action_id=relay_export_feedback, URL opens the feedback export endpoint)
If no feedback_rows: single section 'No corrections this week — great accuracy!'
build_home() accepts optional feedback_rows: list[Any] = None; appends _accuracy_blocks after impact blocks
publish_app_home queries SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE workspace_id=? AND created_at >= now()-7d LIMIT 500
Also queries total questions in the same 7-day window: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM questions WHERE workspace_id=? AND created_at >= now()-7d
Unit test: _accuracy_blocks with 2 mark_not_question corrections and 20 total questions returns '90.0% accuracy'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The export button URL: {settings.app_base_url}/relay/admin/feedback-export — just a link button (type=button with url field). Bolt does not need to handle this action. Keep the accuracy calculation as simple integer math.

userStories.title: Admin feedback export endpoint
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to download this week's feedback signals as a JSONL file for classifier retraining.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/api/admin.py implements GET /relay/admin/feedback-export FastAPI endpoint
Accepts optional ?days=7 query param (default 7, max 90, clamped)
Requires Authorization: Bearer <slack_bot_token> header; resolves workspace from the token's team_id via the Slack API (auth.test)
Requires relay_role='admin' on the requesting user (looked up by slack_user_id from auth.test); returns HTTP 403 otherwise
Returns StreamingResponse with Content-Type: application/x-ndjson and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=relay-feedback-{date}.jsonl

Each JSONL line: {workspace_id, question_id, draft_id, message_id, correction_action, original_label, corrected_label, original_confidence, notes, created_at} — all fields from the feedback_signals row
RLS enforced: endpoint only sees feedback_signals for the authenticated workspace
Unit test: mock Slack auth.test returning a team_id + user_id; verify 403 for non-admin; verify JSONL output for admin
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The FastAPI app is at relay/api/app.py. Add the admin router there. Use StreamingResponse with a generator that yields one JSON line per row. The Slack bot token for the workspace is in workspace_tokens — look it up by team_id. For auth.test call, use httpx.AsyncClient or the Slack SDK's WebClient.

userStories.title: /relay pulse slash command — account health digest
userStories.description: As a CSM or manager, I want to run /relay pulse [account-name] to see open questions, SLA performance, and CRM signals for an account.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/commands/pulse.py implements handle_pulse(ack, respond, command) Bolt handler
With no argument: returns Block Kit with top-5 accounts by open question count (sorted descending), each showing: account name, open count, tier, renewal proximity, primary/backup owner
With account-name argument: case-insensitive substring match on customer_accounts.name; if not found: 'Account not found. Run /relay register to add it.'

Per-account pulse block shows: open question count (state IN (open, detected, claimed)), SLA met rate (30d from impact_metrics), last resolved question date, renewal proximity, ARR, tier, backup owner if primary User.is_ooo=True
Response is ephemeral, formatted as Block Kit sections with header
Slash command registered: relay/commands/help.py dispatches 'pulse' subcommand to handle_pulse
relay/slack/app.py imports pulse handler
Unit tests in tests/test_pulse_command.py: no-arg returns top accounts list, with matching name returns account pulse, non-matching name returns not-found message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Renewal proximity: (renewal_date - today).days formatted as '30d away' or 'OVERDUE (5d ago)' — reuse _renewal_proximity from draft_modal.py. The SLA met rate query: SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN sla_met THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*) FROM impact_metrics WHERE workspace_id=? AND question_id IN (SELECT id FROM questions WHERE channel_id IN (...)) AND created_at >= now()-30d.
